title: Share of service in total exports
subtitle: Services refer to economic output of intangible commodities that may be produced, transferred, and consumed at the same time. The share of services in total exports comes from comparing trade in goods and services at current US dollars.
caption:
Share of service in total exports

Services refer to economic output of intangible commodities that may be produced, transferred, and consumed at the same time. The share of services in total exports comes from comparing trade in goods and services at current US dollars.

The chart presents a vertical axis scaled from 0% to 100% in increments of 20%, intended to display the share of service in total exports for the World. The data source is listed as the International Monetary Fund via the World Bank, with a reference year of 2026. Despite the presence of the axis and title, the central plotting area contains no bars, lines, or data points. Instead, a text message centered in the middle of the graph states "No data for the selected countries and regions." Consequently, no specific values, trends, or comparisons between regions are visible within the frame. The subtitle clarifies that the metric measures economic output of intangible commodities relative to total trade in goods and services at current US dollars, but the visual component of the chart remains empty, offering no quantitative information to observe regarding the distribution or magnitude of service exports globally.
